Orchestrator (Meta-Agent) - Unlike the other agents, the Orchestrator does not generate debate content. Instead, it acts as the "brain" of your routing system, evaluating the current state of the conversation and programmatically deciding which agent should speak next, what they should focus on, and when the debate should end.
Probes assumptions and uncovers hidden premises through systematic Socratic questioning.

A raw troll prompt produces noise. A red-team troll produces labeled noise that trains your other agents to recognize and neutralize bad-faith rhetoric. The Tactic Ledger turns disruption into a teaching signal (English)

A formal, evidence-based debate style. Agents cite sources, use precise language, and maintain scholarly decorum.
A confrontational, emotionally charged debate style. Agents challenge each other aggressively and use rhetorical devices.
A balanced, objective debate style. Agents present facts and arguments without emotional coloring.

A debate workflow using the Socratic method for deep examination of claims.

A comprehensive multi-phase debate workflow with five consecutive phases: Problem Framing, Position Building, Stress Testing, Integration, and Closure. Uses 15 specialized agent bundles in structured phase containers.

A three-stage dialectical workflow following Hegel's method. The Strategist formulates the thesis (These), the Critic develops the antithesis (Antithese), and the Optimizer synthesizes both into a higher unity (synthesis/Aufhebung). Supports multiple rounds of dialectical refinement.

An interview-style workflow where a Strategist conducts structured inquiry through an Analyst, with a Moderator ensuring focus and summarizing findings. Ideal for investigative analysis and information gathering.
A structured ethical analysis using Kantian principles. The strategist proposes, the critic applies the categorical imperative, and the moderator synthesizes.

A conflict resolution workflow where a Mediator facilitates dialogue between opposing parties (represented by Strategist and Critic perspectives). The Mediator guides toward common ground and a mutually acceptable resolution.
A lightweight single-agent review with optional user injection. Fast turnaround for simple tasks.
A classic multi-agent debate with strategist, critic, optimizer, and moderator. Suitable for most analytical tasks.

A confrontational debate format where an Advocate defends one position while an Adversary attacks it. A Referee ensures fair play, and the Moderator synthesizes the outcome. Based on the adversarial method of philosophical inquiry.
